qa_pairs.question: After loading a COMSOL module in JupyterLab, how do I start the graphical interface?
qa_pairs.answer: After clicking 'Load' for the highlighted COMSOL module in JupyterLab, a 'Comsol (VNC)' icon will appear on your desktop. Clicking this icon will automatically start COMSOL in a remote Jupyter desktop.

qa_pairs.question: What method is used to solve parametric problems interactively within the COMSOL GUI?
qa_pairs.answer: Parametric problems can be solved using the 'Batch Sweep' approach when working interactively in the COMSOL graphical user interface.

qa_pairs.question: Can multiple parameter sweeps be performed using the Batch Sweep approach in COMSOL?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, multiple parameter sweeps can be carried out using the Batch Sweep approach.

qa_pairs.question: How can speedup be achieved when performing batch sweeps in COMSOL?
qa_pairs.answer: Speedup due to Task Parallelism may be realized when performing batch sweeps.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure for running a parameter sweep on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a parameter sweep on a cluster, a job must be submitted to the scheduler from the command line using the `sbatch slurmscript` command.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find detailed information regarding additional arguments required for cluster sweeps?
qa_pairs.answer: For a discussion regarding additional required arguments for cluster sweeps, users can refer to the details provided in reference 'a' (https://www.comsol.com/support/knowledgebase/1250) and 'b' (https://www.comsol.com/blogs/how-to-use-job-sequences-to-save-data-after-solving-your-model/).

qa_pairs.question: Is it currently possible to submit parametric simulations to the cluster queue from the graphical interface using a Cluster Sweep node?
qa_pairs.answer: No, support for submitting parametric simulations to the cluster queue from the graphical interface using a Cluster Sweep node is not available at this time.
